Employee.title: Employee
Employee.description: Represents an employee in the system.

employeeId.description: The employee's unique ID. This is to represent the ID in the company.

avatar.description: URL to the employee's avatar image.

role.description: The role of the user in the system.

ServiceRecord.title: ServiceRecord
ServiceRecord.description: Represents a service record for an employee.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service record.

arrivalDateTime.description: Date and time of arrival for the service.

departureDateTime.description: Date and time of departure for the service.

pendingTasks.description: Description of pending tasks for the service.

media.description: Array of media objects (images or videos) as base64 data URLs.

albarans.description: List of 'albaran' numbers associated with the service record.

definition.description: Stores employee profiles. The employeeId is the document ID.  Security rules ensure only the authenticated employee can access their profile.
Stores service records for each employee. Provides path-based authorization. Only the employee who 'owns' the service record can access it.

params.name: serviceRecordId
params.description: Unique identifier for the service record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the 'Registre Diari de Treball' application, focusing on simplicity, security, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for employee data and nested service records. The 'photos' collection has been replaced by a 'media' array within the ServiceRecord document to store base64 data URLs of images and videos directly. This simplifies data retrieval for each service record, as it avoids extra subcollection reads, but may increase document size. This design ensures that security rules can be written clearly and efficiently.

**Authorization Independence and QAPs:**

*   **Employees Collection:** The `/employees/{employeeId}` collection stores employee profiles. Security rules will ensure only the authenticated employee (or an admin) can access their own profile.
*   **Service Records:** Each employee has a subcollection `/employees/{employeeId}/serviceRecords/{serviceRecordId}`. This structure provides path-based authorization. Only the employee who 'owns' the service record can access it.

This design guarantees that list operations are always secure (QAPs), as security rules can filter based on the employeeId path segment.
